instructions.instruction: Inspect the repository's wrapper, Java release/toolchain, dependency management, Quarkus BOM/extensions, modules, generated sources, tests, and deployment targets before editing.
instructions.original_snippets: Inspect before prescribing; the repository is the version source of truth.
instructions.relevant_when: Any Java or Quarkus repository task

instructions.instruction: Treat Java 21 as a capability baseline, never as permission to upgrade a project targeting another release.
instructions.original_snippets: Do not force Java 21 onto a project that targets another release.

instructions.instruction: Capture equality, hashing, ordering, mutability, nullness, serialization, exceptions, transactions, threads, time/locale, and wire behavior as contracts before risky refactors.
instructions.original_snippets: Do not hide behavior changes inside cleanup.

instructions.instruction: Use the smallest defensible patch and exclude unrelated formatting, dependency upgrades, package moves, framework migrations, and abstractions.
instructions.original_snippets: Avoid drive-by churn.

instructions.instruction: Never edit generated output; modify the generator, schema, annotation-processor input, migration source, or template and regenerate.
instructions.original_snippets: Do not edit generated output.

instructions.instruction: Label scanner matches as candidates until semantic context proves a defect.
instructions.original_snippets: Heuristic scanner findings are candidates, not proof.

instructions.instruction: Use records only when transparent data/value semantics, generated equality, and shallow component exposure are correct.
instructions.original_snippets: Use records only for transparent data carriers whose generated equality and component exposure are correct.

instructions.instruction: Check mutability, null rejection, order, and duplicate policy before changing collection factories, collectors, or Stream.toList.
instructions.original_snippets: Changing collectors or copies can change mutability/null/order semantics.

instructions.instruction: Set charset, locale, zone, clock, rounding, timeout, and resource ownership explicitly at external boundaries.
instructions.original_snippets: Set charsets, timeouts, locale, zone, rounding, and resource lifetime explicitly.
instructions.relevant_when: I/O, numeric, time, or network work

instructions.instruction: Preserve exception causes and interruption; avoid empty/broad catches and duplicate log-and-rethrow patterns.
instructions.original_snippets: Do not swallow, log-and-rethrow, or catch Throwable casually.

instructions.instruction: For every executor, future, virtual-thread, or reactive path, define owner, bounds, cancellation, context, timeout, downstream capacity, and observability.
instructions.original_snippets: Do not introduce concurrency without an execution and lifecycle model.

instructions.instruction: Use virtual threads for suitable blocking I/O, do not pool them, do not assume CPU speedup, and make pinning advice JDK-specific.
instructions.original_snippets: Pooling virtual threads or assuming they make CPU-bound work faster is a failure trap.

instructions.instruction: Place transactions around coherent business state transitions and verify interception, flush, rollback, lazy loading, external side effects, retries, and asynchronous completion.
instructions.original_snippets: Moving a transaction boundary changes semantic behavior.

instructions.instruction: Use database constraints for persisted invariants; count-then-insert checks alone are racy.
instructions.original_snippets: Database constraints protect invariants under concurrency.

instructions.instruction: In Quarkus, identify augmentation/build-time behavior, configuration phase, bean indexing/discovery, unused-bean removal, and native implications.
instructions.original_snippets: Treat CDI discovery, build-time configuration, and native behavior as semantics.

instructions.instruction: Prefer constructor injection for mandatory Quarkus dependencies when supported, choose scopes by lifecycle/concurrency, and avoid routine @Named qualifiers.
instructions.original_snippets: Constructor injection and type-safe qualifiers improve ArC correctness.

instructions.instruction: Trace Quarkus REST signatures and filters to the actual event-loop, worker, reactive, or virtual-thread model; never hide blocking calls in reactive wrappers.
instructions.original_snippets: Never perform blocking work on an event loop.

instructions.instruction: Keep Hibernate ORM/JDBC and Hibernate Reactive transaction/execution models consistent per path; do not block-await reactive persistence.
instructions.original_snippets: Do not mix blocking and reactive persistence casually.

instructions.instruction: Distinguish build-time-fixed and runtime-overridable Quarkus configuration and prove runtime overrides against a packaged artifact.
instructions.original_snippets: Build-time properties cannot be assumed runtime-switchable.

instructions.instruction: Keep JPA entities out of REST/message contracts unless the coupling is an explicit accepted design.
instructions.original_snippets: Entities should not be public transport contracts by convenience.

instructions.instruction: Treat messaging acknowledgment, redelivery, ordering, idempotency, and database/message atomicity as explicit distributed-system contracts.
instructions.original_snippets: A Java transaction does not automatically make broker and database atomic.

instructions.instruction: Use plain unit, component, Quarkus, packaged integration, and native tests only for contracts each layer can prove.
instructions.original_snippets: Match the test layer to the behavior under test.

instructions.instruction: Run gates in widening circles using checked-in wrappers, and report exact commands/results without claiming unavailable Docker/native/security gates passed.
instructions.original_snippets: Never claim an unrun gate passed.

instructions.instruction: For native failures, identify exact reflection/resource/class-initialization edges and avoid registering whole packages before proving need.
instructions.original_snippets: Broad reflection registration is a failure trap.

instructions.instruction: Migrate Spring to Quarkus by vertical-slice contracts, not mass annotation replacement.
instructions.original_snippets: Quarkus is not Spring with different annotations.

instructions.instruction: Assess security with separate authentication, authorization/ownership, validation/canonicalization, limits, secrets, and safe failure behavior.
instructions.original_snippets: Validation alone does not provide authorization.
